name: Subscription auto-renewal charge
summary: Recurring billing that continues until the user affirmatively cancels.

evidence.claim: California SB-313 requires online-signup subscriptions to permit online cancellation.
The federal FTC Click-to-Cancel Rule was vacated by the 8th Circuit on July 8 2025; state-level protections remain in force.

body.description: A charge that recurs on a periodic basis (monthly, annually) until the user affirmatively cancels. The user's explicit consent at signup is required; the cancellation mechanism must match the signup channel in states with click-to-cancel laws (E1).

disclosureLegality.requirement: Must be disclosed before charge; cancellation channel must match signup channel (E1).
Federal Click-to-Cancel Rule vacated July 2025 (E2). No current federal requirement.

negotiability.script: I want to cancel the auto-renewal on my subscription. Per [applicable state law], I'm entitled to cancel through the same channel I signed up through. Please confirm cancellation in writing.
